name: igNUS 2027
writeup: igNUS 2027 is currently in the early design phase. Building on lessons learned from our 2026 campaign, the team is exploring a next-generation chassis architecture, enhanced perception systems, and a redesigned robotic arm with improved dexterity.
upcomingFeatures: Next-generation chassis with improved terrain adaptability
Enhanced perception suite for autonomous navigation
Upgraded science payload with expanded detection capabilities

name: igNUS 2026
writeup: igNUS 2026 represents our most ambitious build to date. Featuring a rugged new chassis designed for the Utah desert terrain, a more versatile 6-DOF robotic arm, cutting-edge autonav with improved obstacle avoidance, advanced GNSS and communications systems, and an upgraded science payload capable of detecting signs of past or present life.

name: igNUS 2025
writeup: igNUS 2025 was our most refined rover at the time of competition, featuring autonomous GPS-guided navigation, a 6-DOF robotic arm for equipment servicing tasks, and a science mission payload designed for soil analysis and life detection. The team submitted a System Acceptance Review video for URC 2025.
